Study the four statements (A-D) given below and select the two correct ones out of them:

 The definition of biological species was given by Ernst Mayr.

 The binomial nomenclature system was given by R.H. Whittaker.
 In unicellular organisms, reproduction is synonymous with growth.

 The two correct statements are :

  4. In unicellular organisms, reproduction is synonymous with growth.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is not correct?

 Biodiversity is the occurrence of variety of life forms differing in morphology, anatomy, habitats and habits.
 Systematics is the branch of biology that deals with cataloguing plants, animals and other organisms into categories that can be named, remembered, compared and studied.
 Classification is the branch of biology that deals with principles and procedures of Identification and nomenclature of organisms.
 Statement a and b
questions.question_html:
Which of the following statement is not correct?

  1. Biodiversity is the occurrence of variety of life forms differing in morphology, anatomy, habitats and habits.
  2. Systematics is the branch of biology that deals with cataloguing plants, animals and other organisms into categories that can be named, remembered, compared and studied.
  3. Classification is the branch of biology that deals with principles and procedures of  Identification and nomenclature of organisms.
  4. Statement a and b

questions.question:
A type is one particular specimen (or a group of specimens) of an organism to which the scientific. name of that organism is formally attached. Match column I (type) with column II (description) and select the correct option from the codes given below:

 
 
 Column I

 (Type)
 Column II

 (Description)
 
 
 (A) Holotype
 (i) A specimen cited with an original description other than the holotype or isotype.
 
 
 (B) Isotype
 (ii) A duplicate of the holotype.
 
 
 (C) Paratype
 (iii) A specimen designated in the original description.
 
 
 (D) Lectotype
 (iv) A specimen selected from original material to serve as nomenclatural type when the holotype was not designated.
questions.question_html:
A type is one particular specimen (or a group of specimens) of an organism to which the scientific. name of that organism is formally attached. Match column I (type) with column II (description) and select the correct option from the codes given below:

Column I
(Type)		 Column II
(Description)
(A) Holotype (i) A specimen cited with an original description other than the holotype or isotype.
(B) Isotype (ii) A duplicate of the holotype.
(C) Paratype (iii) A specimen designated in the original description.
(D) Lectotype (iv) A specimen selected from original material to serve as nomenclatural type when the holotype was not designated.

Atomic $\rightarrow$ Molecular $\rightarrow$ Subcellular $\rightarrow$ Cellular $\rightarrow$ Tissue $\rightarrow$ Organ system $\rightarrow$ Individual

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is incorrect?

 Term Nothospecies refers to the naturally occurring interspecific hybrids.
 As the species do not change with time, therefore they are considered as static groups.
 Metabolism and response to external stimuli are considered as the defining properties of living organisms.
 Hibiscus rosasinensis belongs to the same family to which Althaea rosea and Gossypium hirsutum belong to.
questions.question_html:
Which of the following statements is incorrect?

  1. Term Nothospecies refers to the naturally occurring interspecific hybrids.
  2. As the species do not change with time, therefore they are considered as static groups.
  3. Metabolism and response to external stimuli are considered as the defining properties of living organisms.
  4. Hibiscus rosasinensis belongs to the same family to which Althaea rosea and Gossypium hirsutum belong to.

questions.question:
Which of the following combinations of statements is correct?

 The taxonomic hierarchy for Brassica campestris can be written as:

 Plantae $\rightarrow$ Phanerogamae $\rightarrow$ Angiospermae $\rightarrow$ Dicotyledonae $\rightarrow$ Parietales $\rightarrow$ Brassicaceae $\rightarrow$ Brassica $\rightarrow$ campestris
 Tautonym is the taxonomic designation used for certain plants having trinomial nomenclature.
 A character present in an ancestral species and shared exclusively by its evolutionary descendants is referred to as synapomorphy.
 Family Fabaceae is divided into three sub-families i.e., Leguminosae, Mimosaceae and Caesalpiniaceae.
questions.question_html:
Which of the following combinations of statements is correct?

  1. The taxonomic hierarchy for Brassica campestris can be written as:
    Plantae {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Phanerogamae {tex}\rightarrow{/tex}  Angiospermae {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Dicotyledonae {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Parietales {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Brassicaceae{tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Brassica {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} campestris
  2. Tautonym is the taxonomic designation used for certain plants having trinomial nomenclature.
  3. A character present in an ancestral species and shared exclusively by its evolutionary descendants is referred to as synapomorphy.
  4. Family Fabaceae is divided into three sub-families i.e., Leguminosae, Mimosaceae and Caesalpiniaceae.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is incorrect?

 All animals belonging to various phyla are assigned to the Kingdom Animalia.
 As we go higher from species to kingdom, number of common characteristics goes on increasing.
 Different classes comprising fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals together constitute the Phylum Chordata.
 Plant order Polymoniales includes the families like Solanaceae and Convolvulaceae based on the vegetative and floral characters.
questions.question_html:
Which of the following statements is incorrect?

  1. All animals belonging to various phyla are assigned to the Kingdom Animalia.
  2. As we go higher from species to kingdom, number of common characteristics goes on increasing.
  3. Different classes comprising fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds and mammals together constitute the Phylum Chordata.
  4. Plant order Polymoniales includes the families like Solanaceae and Convolvulaceae based on the vegetative and floral characters.

questions.question:
Which of the following taxonomic category is being described by the statements given below (i-iii)?

 It is the basic unit of classification.
 It is defined as the group of individuals which resemble in their morphological and reproductive characters and interbreed amongst themselves and produce fertile off springs.
 Human beings belong to the sapiens which is grouped in the genus Homo.
questions.question_html:
Which of the following taxonomic category is being described by the statements given below (i-iii)?

  1. It is the basic unit of classification.
  2. It is defined as the group of individuals which resemble in their morphological and reproductive characters and interbreed amongst themselves and produce fertile off springs.
  3. Human beings belong to the sapiens which is grouped in the genus Homo.
